purpose: Remedy arm 1 for the core 150-episode benchmark: constant-velocity obstacle propagation through the prediction horizon with the uncertainty tube, replacing the paper's static-horizon assumption. The tube contracts once the observer has two distinct sensor updates, so it is far tighter than the worst-case dead-time ball.
references: arXiv:2208.03529 (propagating obstacle dynamics through the horizon)
notes: Velocity is estimated from the 0.67 s zero-order-hold measurements (known_obstacle_velocity stays false); before two distinct updates the tube uses the initial velocity error bound, so early-episode behavior is conservative and then tightens.
All tube parameters keep the SmoothDynamicConfig defaults (confidence_multiplier 3.0, velocity_error_bound 0.03, initial 0.20, model_error_growth 0.005, sampled-data margin on).
This is a deliberate deviation from the paper's static-horizon assumption (registry 2.1); the paper-faithful baseline remains the frozen arm.
